sutta: monks endowed with 5 qualities among may well talk to his fellows in the holy life what 5 monks hearing among in himself has achieved a virtue and explains a question raised by a talk on the achieving of virtue similarly for concentration inside liberation and in himself has achieved the knowledge and insight of liberation and explains a question raised by a talk on the achieving of knowledge and insight of liberation verily monks endowed with these 5 qualities among may well talk to his fellows on in the holy life that's the end of the sutta
commentary: basically what this suta is trying to say is if a monk were to talk about virtue or concentration or insider or liberation or knowledge and insight of liberation he should be preferably be experienced in these things if he's not experienced in these things it would be difficult for him to explain i thought it would be difficult for him to explain well and to explain correctly these different things so that is why if a person has a teen then he would be better qualified to teacher but then you see like during the buddha's time there were arahants around so if there were arahants around the best teachers would be the arahants and when the arahants are not around then of course you have to start going down the scale to see whether you can get arya's to teach and if you can't get arias to teach then then you have to get people who can teach from theory so um we have to see what kind of teachers we can get
